prevalence.trend: stable; chronic urticaria recognized more often

incidence.source: Primary care and allergy clinic data

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant itch, sleep disturbance, and QOL impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical wheals lasting <24h; for chronic urticaria, evaluate duration >6 weeks and triggers

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Millions with acute episodes; hundreds of thousands with chronic disease
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improved with guideline-based antihistamine escalation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; limited lab work-up for chronic cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Second-generation antihistamines, dose escalation if needed; omalizumab for refractory chronic urticaria where available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access to specialist allergy/dermatology care and biologics for refractory cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Antihistamines widely available and effective for most patients
